llm_text:
{
  "recommendations": [
    {
      "rule_id": "rsu-double-count-001",
      "finding_id": "finding-rsu-double-count-001-RSU-VEST-001_RSU-VEST-002_RSU-VEST-003",
      "one_line_summary": "3 RSU lots with ~$0 basis are double-counting W-2 income as capital gain — fix for ~$11,520 refund impact",
      "detail": "W-2 Box 12 Code V already includes vest income in wages; the 1099-B basis must equal that inclusion, not zero. Correcting all 3 lots eliminates the double-count. Prior notes flagged RSU basis as a pain point last year — this is the same class of error.",
      "confidence": 0.95,
      "llm_only": false,
      "goal_alignment_note": "Highest dollar refund impact of any finding (~$11,520 estimate). Fixing over-reported gain directly maximizes refund and simultaneously reduces audit exposure since the corrected return better matches W-2/1099-B IRS matching."
    },
    {
      "rule_id": "salt-cap-tcja-2025-001",
      "finding_id": "finding-salt-cap-tcja-2025-001-global",
      "one_line_summary": "TY2025 SALT cap raised to $40K MFJ — recover ~$2,979 by deducting full $22,412 state/property tax",
      "detail": "The TCJA permanent extension raises the MFJ SALT cap from $10,000 to $40,000 for TY2025. The Mitchells paid $22,412 in state and property taxes, all of which is now deductible. Verify Schedule A line 5e reflects the new cap.",
      "confidence": 0.92,
      "llm_only": false,
      "goal_alignment_note": "Second-largest refund driver (~$2,979). A straightforward cap change that is mechanical to apply and carries low audit risk."
    },
    {
      "rule_id": "wash-sale-code-w-001",
      "finding_id": "finding-wash-sale-code-w-001-WASH-001_WASH-002_WASH-003",
      "one_line_summary": "3 Form 8949 lots missing Code W — $4,180 disallowed loss not reported; high CP2000 risk",
      "detail": "Broker-reported wash sale disallowances are not carried into Form 8949 column (f)/(g). The IRS computer match will flag this. Adding Code W and the $4,180 addback is required for compliance and prevents a CP2000 notice, which is a top audit-risk concern.",
      "confidence": 0.97,
      "llm_only": false,
      "goal_alignment_note": "High audit risk delta (+0.35) makes this the top priority for goal 2 (minimize audit risk). Fixing it also correctly preserves replacement-lot basis for future refund benefit."
    },
    {
      "rule_id": "hsa-8889-reconcile-001",
      "finding_id": "finding-hsa-8889-reconcile-001-person-olivia-mitchell",
      "one_line_summary": "Form 8889 uses $8,300 family limit but TY2025 limit is $8,550 — Olivia may be leaving ~$1,056 on the table",
      "detail": "The TY2025 HSA family contribution limit is $8,550, but Form 8889 line 6 shows $8,300. Correcting this allows an additional $250 deductible contribution. Confirm actual contributions and update lines 2, 6, and 13 to reconcile.",
      "confidence": 0.93,
      "llm_only": false,
      "goal_alignment_note": "Directly increases deduction and refund (~$1,056 estimated impact). A mechanical statutory limit update with low audit risk."
    },
    {
      "rule_id": "depreciation-land-split-001",
      "finding_id": "finding-depreciation-land-split-001-rental-joliet-001",
      "one_line_summary": "Joliet rental depreciating land — pull Will County assessor records to correct basis before filing",
      "detail": "No land/building split exists in the depreciation schedule, so the full purchase price is being depreciated. Land is never depreciable. A 25% land allocation implies ~$2,818 annual over-deduction, creating a compliance error that inflates current deductions and understates recapture later.",
      "confidence": 0.91,
      "llm_only": false,
      "goal_alignment_note": "Correcting this reduces audit risk (delta -0.25) and prevents accumulated recapture surprises. Aligns with goal 2 and protects goal 3 (future-year optimization)."
    },
    {
      "rule_id": "estimated-tax-safe-harbor-110-001",
      "finding_id": "finding-estimated-tax-safe-harbor-110-001-global",
      "one_line_summary": "Withholding $6,352 short of 110% prior-year safe harbor — make Q4 payment to avoid §6654 penalty",
      "detail": "AGI over $150K requires 110% of prior-year tax ($62,502). Only $56,150 is withheld. A $6,352 Q4 estimated payment or wage withholding adjustment eliminates the underpayment penalty (~$508) and satisfies the safe harbor.",
      "confidence": 0.94,
      "llm_only": false,
      "goal_alignment_note": "Directly prevents a $508 penalty and audit risk flag. Supports goal 2 (minimize audit risk) and goal 3 (proactive planning before year-end)."
    },
    {
      "rule_id": "retirement-401k-headroom-003",
      "finding_id": "finding-retirement-401k-headroom-003-person-ryan-mitchell",
      "one_line_summary": "Ryan has $17,500 of unused 401(k) headroom — deferring it saves ~$4,200 in taxes",
      "detail": "Ryan contributed only $6,000 of the $23,500 §402(g) limit. Maximizing before year-end defers $17,500 of ordinary income, saving an estimated $4,200 at their marginal rate. Coordinate with payroll before the last pay period.",
      "confidence": 0.88,
      "llm_only": false,
      "goal_alignment_note": "Largest next-year optimization lever (~$4,200). Aligns with goal 3 and supports goal 1 by reducing taxable income."
    },
    {
      "rule_id": "passive-469-agi-phaseout-001",
      "finding_id": "finding-passive-469-agi-phaseout-001-global",
      "one_line_summary": "Rental loss $6,503 fully suspended at AGI $325,850 — carryforward confirmed, no current deduction available",
      "detail": "The §469(i) $25K rental allowance phases out completely above $150K AGI. The loss is correctly suspended and carries forward. CPA should document the carryforward balance and consider real estate professional status (see separate finding) to unlock it.",
      "confidence": 0.96,
      "llm_only": false,
      "goal_alignment_note": "Ensures the return is correctly prepared (audit risk delta -0.30). Carryforward preservation supports goal 3 — future deduction when passive income offsets become available."
    },
    {
      "rule_id": "passive-rep-consider-004",
      "finding_id": "finding-passive-rep-consider-004-rental-joliet-001",
      "one_line_summary": "If either spouse logs 750+ hours in real property businesses, rental losses become fully deductible — worth ~$1,000",
      "detail": "IRC §469(c)(7) real-estate-professional status removes the passive loss limitation entirely. Document hours meticulously with contemporaneous logs. This is an audit-sensitive election at high AGI, so evidence quality is critical before claiming.",
      "confidence": 0.72,
      "llm_only": false,
      "goal_alignment_note": "Potential to unlock $6,503 suspended rental loss. Moderate audit risk delta (+0.10) means documentation quality is essential — balances goal 1 and goal 2."
    },
    {
      "rule_id": "retirement-401k-headroom-003",
      "finding_id": "finding-retirement-401k-headroom-003-person-olivia-mitchell",
      "one_line_summary": "Olivia has $9,500 of unused 401(k)/403(b) headroom — deferring saves ~$2,280",
      "detail": "Olivia has contributed $14,000 of the $23,500 limit. An additional $9,500 deferred before year-end reduces taxable income by $9,500, saving approximately $2,280 at their marginal rate.",
      "confidence": 0.88,
      "llm_only": false,
      "goal_alignment_note": "Supports goal 3 (next-year optimization) and reduces current-year AGI, which may also ease phaseout calculations. Zero audit risk."
    },
    {
      "rule_id": "salt-cap-ptet-interaction-002",
      "finding_id": "finding-salt-cap-ptet-interaction-002-global",
      "one_line_summary": "PTET elections in IL and CA can convert capped SALT into an uncapped entity-level deduction on K-1 income",
      "detail": "IRS Notice 2020-75 allows states to tax at the entity level, reducing K-1 income by the state tax paid — effectively bypassing the SALT cap. Review both the IL and CA K-1s with the partnerships to determine if elections are available and beneficial.",
      "confidence": 0.80,
      "llm_only": false,
      "goal_alignment_note": "Estimated $1,500 federal benefit from PTET structuring. Primarily a goal 3 (optimize next year) lever since elections must be made at the entity level prospectively."
    },
    {
      "rule_id": "ftc-small-election-002",
      "finding_id": "finding-ftc-small-election-002-global",
      "one_line_summary": "Claim $260 foreign tax credit directly on Schedule 3 without Form 1116 — saves filing complexity",
      "detail": "Total foreign tax is $260, under the $600 MFJ de minimis threshold under IRC §904(k). Drop Form 1116 and claim the credit on Schedule 3 line 1 directly. Simpler, lower audit risk, same dollar result.",
      "confidence": 0.95,
      "llm_only": false,
      "goal_alignment_note": "Small but certain $260 credit with audit risk delta of -0.05. Simplifying the return reduces complexity and marginally supports goal 2."
    }
  ]
}
